zh.lifeSummary: 當Matilda of England Holy Roman Empress於1102年2月7日出生在英國奧可福爾時，她的父親Henry I King of England35歲，母親Edith of Scotland Queen Matilda of England22歲。她於1114年1月6日與Heinrich V Kaiser des Heiligen Römischen Reiches在海利格斯 洛米設斯 萊奇艾爾茲比斯圖姆 麥音麥音結婚。她去世於1167年9月10日在法國諾門迪瑟-馬利盧享年65歲，埋葬在法國諾門迪瑟-馬利盧。

nameMeanings.name: of
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Chinese: Cheng, Jing, Kam Fung, Wai Shing, Wing, Yali, Yuk.
variant Romanization of the surnames 歐 and 區, see   Ou   1 and 2.

variants.name: Ou
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Chinese Wei, Ying, Ching, Heng, Hong, Jia, Kuang Yu, Li-Ching, Cho, Chong, Chung, Sa.  Southeast Asian Lichun, Ming, Quan, Roeun, Sokhom, Chau, Hai, Leang, Ly, Nhanh, Phan, Pho, Tha.

Chinese: Mandarin form of the surname 歐, meaning ‘to sing someone's praise’ in ancient Chinese: (i) from the placename Dong Ou (東甌), a fief (in present-day Wenzhou in Zhejiang province) which was granted to Yao in 192  BC . It is said that Yao was a descendant of Wujiang (reigned 355–333  BC ), king of the state of Yue (located mainly in present-day Zhejiang and Jiangxi provinces) during the Spring and Autumn period (770–476  BC ). (ii) from the placename Ou Yu (歐餘), the name of a fief (located in present-day Zhejiang province) that was granted to Ti, the second son of Wujiang (reigned 355–333  BC ), king of the state of Yue (located mainly in present-day Zhejiang and Jiangxi provinces) during the Spring and Autumn period (770–476  BC ).

Chinese: Mandarin form of the surname 區, originally denoting an ancient vessel for measurement in ancient Chinese: from the personal name of Ou Zhizi (歐治子), an expert sword-maker who lived during the Spring and Autumn period (770–476 BC ). Some of his descendants inherited Ou (歐) as their surname, simplifying the Chinese character 歐 to 區.

nameMeanings.name: England
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English: habitational name from Middle English Engelond ‘England’. It was probably a formal alternative to English , which is also well evidenced as a surname in England. These names may have been acquired by English landowners who moved in Norman social circles or who lived in a neighbouring country (Scotland, Wales, or Ireland), or by English merchants who traded abroad.
Norwegian: habitational name from any of various farmsteads so named, from Old Norse eng ‘meadow’ + land ‘land’.
Swedish: ornamental name with the same meaning as 2.

variants.name: English
meanings: English, Scottish, and Welsh: ethnic name from Middle English English, Inglish, Inglis ‘English’ (Old English Englisc), sometimes alternating with Anglo-Norman French Engleis, Engles, Anglais, Angles (Old French Englois). Compare Inglis and England . Among the aristocracy and upper gentry it marked out a man of English ancestry from one of Norman or continental origin. In counties bordering England with Scotland and Wales the name distinguished an Englishman from a Scot or a native Welshman on both sides of the border. The name may also have been acquired by English merchants who traded abroad or who lived and worked in a ‘French’ borough in England (one exclusively administered by Normans).
Irish: in Ireland, this name was used to denote an Englishman, often being adopted for Irish Aingléis ‘Englishman’ or through mistranslation for Mac an Ghallóglaigh, see Gallogly and Golightly .

nameMeanings.name: Matilda
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Latinized form of a Germanic personal name derived from maht, meht ‘might’ + hild ‘battle’. This was the name of an early German queen ( 895–968 ), wife of Henry the Fowler, who was noted for her piety and generosity. It was also the name of the wife of William the Conqueror and of the daughter of Henry I of England ( see Maud ). The name was introduced into England by the Normans, and this Latinized form is the one that normally occurs in medieval records, while the vernacular form Maud was the one in everyday use. Matilda was revived in England as a learned form in the 18th century.

variants.name: Mathilda
meanings: Latinized form of a Germanic personal name derived from maht, meht ‘might’ + hild ‘battle’. This was the name of an early German queen ( 895–968 ), wife of Henry the Fowler, who was noted for her piety and generosity. It was also the name of the wife of William the Conqueror and of the daughter of Henry I of England ( see Maud ). The name was introduced into England by the Normans, and this Latinized form is the one that normally occurs in medieval records, while the vernacular form Maud was the one in everyday use. Matilda was revived in England as a learned form in the 18th century.

historicEvents.title: The Battle of Tichebray
historicEvents.description: King Henry I defeated and captured his brother, Robert of Normandy, at the Battle of Tinchebray in 1106. Robert remained in captivity until his death in 1134.

en.lifeSummary: When Matilda of England Holy Roman Empress was born on 7 February 1102, in Oxfordshire, England, her father, Henry I King of England, was 35 and her mother, Edith of Scotland Queen Matilda of England, was 22. She married Heinrich V Kaiser des Heiligen Römischen Reiches on 6 January 1114, in Mainz, Erzbistum Mainz, Holy Roman Empire. She died on 10 September 1167, in Rouen, Seine-Maritime, Normandy, France, at the age of 65, and was buried in Rouen, Seine-Maritime, Normandy, France.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Matilda of England Holy Roman Empress est née le 7 février 1102, à Oxfordshire, Angleterre, son père, Henry I King of England, avait 35 ans et sa mère, Edith of Scotland Queen Matilda of England, avait 22 ans. Elle a épousé Heinrich V Kaiser des Heiligen Römischen Reiches le 6 janvier 1114, à Mainz, Erzbistum Mainz, Saint-Empire romain. Elle est décédée le 10 septembre 1167, à Rouen, Seine-Maritime, Normandie, France, à l’âge de 65 ans, et a été enterrée à Rouen, Seine-Maritime, Normandie, France.

de.lifeSummary: Als Matilda of England Holy Roman Empress am 7. Februar 1102 in Oxfordshire, England geboren wurde, war ihr Vater Henry I King of England 35 und ihre Mutter Edith of Scotland Queen Matilda of England 22. Sie hat am 6. Januar 1114 in Mainz, Erzbistum Mainz, Heiliges Römisches Reich Heinrich V Kaiser des Heiligen Römischen Reiches geheiratet. Sie starb am 10. September 1167 im Alter von 65 Jahren in Rouen, Seine-Maritime, Normandie, Frankreich und wurde in Rouen, Seine-Maritime, Normandie, Frankreich begraben.

Lei sposato Heinrich V Kaiser des Heiligen Römischen Reiches il 6 gennaio 1114, nel Mainz, Erzbistum Mainz, Heiliges Römisches Reich. ja.Mainz, Erzbistum Mainz, Holy Roman Empir: ヘイリジェズ ルoミシェス レイチエルズビストゥム メインズメインズ
ja.lifeSummary: Matilda of England Holy Roman Empressが1102年2月7日にイギリスオクスフォードシュアイアで生まれた時，父Henry I King of Englandは35歳，母Edith of Scotland Queen Matilda of Englandは22歳であった。1114年1月6日にヘイリジェズ ルoミシェス レイチエルズビストゥム メインズメインズでHeinrich V Kaiser des Heiligen Römischen Reichesと結婚した。彼女は1167年9月10日にフランスノーマンディセアイネ-マリティメロウンで65歳で死去し，フランスノーマンディセアイネ-マリティメロウンにて埋葬された。

ko.Château-du-Loir, Sarthe, Pays de la Loir: 프랑스 파이스 데 라 로이르 사르테 cha테오-드-로이르
ko.Palace of Westminster, Westminster, Midd: 영국 미들레세스 웨스트민스터 팔레이스 프 웨스트민스터
ko.Mainz, Erzbistum Mainz, Holy Roman Empir: 헤이리그스 o미슈스 라이치 르즈비스투 맹츠 맹츠
ko.lifeSummary: Matilda of England Holy Roman Empress(이)가 1102년 2월 7일 영국 스퍼드실르에서 태어났을 때, 그녀의 아버지 Henry I King of England(은)는 35세였고, 그녀의 어머니 Edith of Scotland Queen Matilda of England(은)는 22세였다. 그녀는 1114년 1월 6일 헤이리그스 o미슈스 라이치 르즈비스투 맹츠 맹츠에서 Heinrich V Kaiser des Heiligen Römischen Reiches(와)과 결혼했다. 그녀는 1167년 9월 10일 프랑스 노먼디 세이인-마이팀 루언에서 65세의 나이로 사망했으며 프랑스 노먼디 세이인-마이팀 루언에 묻혔다.

pt.7 February 1102–10 September 1167: 7 de fevereiro de 1102–10 de setembro de 1167
pt.Château-du-Loir, Sarthe, Pays de la Loir: Château-du-Loir, Sarthe, Pays de la Loire, França
pt.Palace of Westminster, Westminster, Midd: Palace of Westminster, Westminster, Middlesex, Inglaterra
pt.Mainz, Erzbistum Mainz, Holy Roman Empir: Mainz, Erzbistum Mainz, Heiliges Römisches Reich
pt.lifeSummary: Quando Matilda of England Holy Roman Empress nasceu em 7 de fevereiro de 1102, em Oxfordshire, Inglaterra, seu pai, Henry I King of England, tinha 35 anos e sua mãe, Edith of Scotland Queen Matilda of England, tinha 22 anos. Ela casou-se com Heinrich V Kaiser des Heiligen Römischen Reiches em 6 de janeiro de 1114, em Mainz, Erzbistum Mainz, Heiliges Römisches Reich. Ela faleceu em 10 de setembro de 1167, em Rouen, Seine-Maritime, Normandie, França, com 65 anos, e foi sepultada em Rouen, Seine-Maritime, Normandie, França.

ru.Palace of Westminster, Westminster, Midd: палеис оф вестминстир, вестминстир, миддлесекс, Англия
ru.Mainz, Erzbistum Mainz, Holy Roman Empir: меинз, эрзбистум меинз, хилигесь рoмищесь раич
ru.lifeSummary: Когда Matilda of England Holy Roman Empress родилась 7 февраля 1102 г. в местности оксфордшир, Англия, её отцу по имени Henry I King of England было 35 лет, а матери, которую звали Edith of Scotland Queen Matilda of England, 22. Она вышла замуж за человека по имени Heinrich V Kaiser des Heiligen Römischen Reiches 6 января 1114 г. в местности меинз, эрзбистум меинз, хилигесь рoмищесь раич. Она умерла 10 сентября 1167 г. в местности роуен, сине-маритим, норманди, Франция в возрасте 65 лет и была похоронена в местности роуен, сине-маритим, норманди, Франция.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Matilda of England Holy Roman Empress nació el 7 de febrero de 1102, en Oxfordshire, Inglaterra, su papá, Henry I King of England, tenía 35 años y su mamá, Edith of Scotland Queen Matilda of England, tenía 22 años. Se casó con Heinrich V Kaiser des Heiligen Römischen Reiches el 6 de enero de 1114, en Mainz, Erzbistum Mainz, Heiliges Römisches Reich. Murió el 10 de septiembre de 1167, en Rouen, Seine-Maritime, Normandie, Francia, a la edad de 65 años, y fue sepultada en Rouen, Seine-Maritime, Normandie, Francia.
